info.name: AgentGateway
info.description: Admin API for AgentGateway. Manage gateways and their registries, policies, consumers, roles and auth credentials. Set the `base_url` and `admin_token` collection variables before running. IDs captured from create responses are stored automatically into `gateway_id`, `registry_id`, `policy_id`, `consumer_id`, `role_id` and `auth_id`. Creating an `api_key` auth returns the generated key exactly once; it is captured into `consumer_api_key`. Use `E2E Flow` for inline + API key, `E2E Flow (OAuth2 JWT)` for inline + classic OAuth Bearer JWT, and `E2E Role-Based Flow (IDP)` for roles + IDP auth.

request.description: Optional `slug` (lowercase DNS label) identifies the gateway on the proxy plane: `X-AG-Gateway-Slug` header (GATEWAY_DISCOVERY_MODE=header, default) or `{slug}.<GATEWAY_BASE_DOMAIN>` host (subdomain mode). Omitted slugs are derived from the name.

item.name: Create Azure Registry (OAuth Service Principal)
item.description: Default Azure auth mode: OAuth via service principal. Requires auth.type=azure, azure.endpoint, azure.tenant_id, azure.client_id, and azure.client_secret. It is not generic auth.type=oauth2.

item.name: Create Azure Registry (API Key)
item.description: Azure API key mode requires auth.type=azure, azure.endpoint, and azure.api_key.

item.name: Create Azure Registry (DefaultAzureCredential)
item.description: The public field use_managed_identity is retained for compatibility, but AgentGateway uses Azure SDK DefaultAzureCredential semantics for this mode.

item.name: Update Azure Registry (Preserve Secret)
item.description: Use the redaction placeholder for the same Azure mode to preserve the stored api_key or client_secret during partial updates.

item.name: Test Connection (OpenAI API Key)
item.description: Tests provider credentials without creating a registry.

item.name: Test Azure Connection (API Key)
item.description: Connection testing for Azure API key auth.

item.name: Test Azure Connection (Service Principal)
item.description: Connection testing supports Azure service principal auth under auth.type=azure.

item.name: Test Azure Connection (DefaultAzureCredential)
item.description: use_managed_identity=true keeps the existing API name, but the runtime uses Azure SDK DefaultAzureCredential.

request.description: `routing_mode` is `inline` (default) or `role_based`. Inline consumers own their registries, model policies, `lb_config` (with optional `pool_alias` addressable as `pool:<alias>` in the payload) and `fallback`. LB and fallback never apply to role_based consumers.

Role-based consumers authorize through roles (attach with POST .../consumers/:id/roles/:role_id) and authenticate via IDP or OAuth2 client credentials. They cannot hold registries, lb_config or fallback.

Partial update. Registries are managed via attach/detach endpoints; `model_policies` here updates policies for already-bound registries. Send `lb_config`/`fallback` with `enabled: false` to clear them.
Only valid for role_based consumers. Returns 409 for inline consumers.

item.name: Roles
item.description: Roles own model/MCP policies and map IDP claims to access. Create the role, attach registries, then update it with model_policies for those registries.

request.description: `model_policies` cannot be set on create: bind registries first, then update the role. `idp_mapping` grants the role to IDP tokens whose claims match.
`model_policies` may only reference registries already attached to the role.

item.name: Auth Credentials

exec: // The plaintext key is generated server-side and shown ONLY here.

request.description: Classic OAuth: validates Bearer JWTs from an external authorization server (JWKS or introspection). Inline consumers only.
The gateway issues tokens via the client_credentials grant. Used by role_based consumers.
Validates IDP-issued JWTs; claims are matched against role idp_mapping rules. Role-based consumers only.
Authenticates clients by TLS certificate against a CA plus CN/DNS/fingerprint allow-lists.

item.description: Gateway auth credentials. `api_key` and `oauth2` apply to inline consumers; `idp` and `oauth2_client` apply to role_based consumers. `oauth2` is classic OAuth (Bearer JWT validation); `oauth2_client` lets the gateway obtain tokens via client_credentials.

item.name: E2E Flow
item.description: End-to-end flow that provisions a gateway with an OpenAI backend, a policy, an API-key credential and a consumer, then sends a real chat-completion through the proxy plane (:8081). Run the requests top-to-bottom (Collection Runner runs them in order). Set the `openai_api_key` variable to a real OpenAI key before running. Step 4 generates the consumer API key server-side and captures it into `consumer_api_key`; step 5a binds the OpenAI registry with model policies at create time; step 6 sends the key in the fixed `X-AG-API-Key` header (no `X-Gateway-Id` is used).

request.description: Optional `slug` (lowercase DNS label) identifies the gateway on the proxy plane: `X-AG-Gateway-Slug` header (GATEWAY_DISCOVERY_MODE=header, default) or `{slug}.<GATEWAY_BASE_DOMAIN>` host (subdomain mode). Omitted slugs are derived from the name.

item.name: 2b. Create Registry (Google)
item.description: Optional provider payload. Does not overwrite registry_id used by later E2E steps.

item.name: 2c. Create Registry (Vertex)
item.description: Optional provider payload. Does not overwrite registry_id used by later E2E steps.

item.name: 2d. Create Registry (Anthropic)
item.description: Optional provider payload. Does not overwrite registry_id used by later E2E steps.

item.name: 2e. Create Registry (Bedrock)
item.description: Optional provider payload. Does not overwrite registry_id used by later E2E steps.

item.name: 2f. Create Registry (Azure OAuth Service Principal)
item.description: Default Azure auth mode: OAuth via service principal (auth.type=azure with tenant_id, client_id, client_secret). Optional provider payload; does not overwrite registry_id used by later E2E steps.

item.name: 2g. Create Registry (Azure API Key)
item.description: Optional provider payload. Does not overwrite registry_id used by later E2E steps.

item.name: 2h. Create Registry (Mistral)
item.description: Optional provider payload. Does not overwrite registry_id used by later E2E steps.

item.name: 2i. Create Registry (Groq)
item.description: Optional provider payload. Does not overwrite registry_id used by later E2E steps.

exec: // The key is generated server-side and only surfaced here; the proxy
// step authenticates with it via the fixed X-AG-API-Key header.

request.description: Binds the OpenAI registry and its model policy atomically via the nested `registries[].model_policies` field. Uses `gpt-4o-mini` to match step 6.
Optional: registry was already bound in step 5a; this attach is idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).

header.description: Gateway discovery in header mode; in subdomain mode use Host {slug}.<GATEWAY_BASE_DOMAIN> instead.

item.name: E2E Flow (OAuth2 JWT)
item.description: End-to-end inline consumer authenticated with classic OAuth2 (Bearer JWT validated via JWKS/introspection). Set `openai_api_key`, `oauth2_issuer`, `oauth2_jwks_url` and `oauth2_bearer_token` before running. oauth2 and oauth2_client are mutually exclusive on the same consumer.

request.description: Classic OAuth: validates Bearer JWTs from an external authorization server. Inline consumers only.

header.description: OAuth2 access token or JWT accepted by the gateway oauth2 auth config.

item.name: E2E Role-Based Flow (IDP)
item.description: End-to-end role-based provisioning: OpenAI registry, role with idp_mapping and model_policies, IDP auth credential, role_based consumer, then a proxy call authenticated with a Bearer JWT. Set `openai_api_key`, `idp_issuer`, `idp_jwks_url` and `idp_bearer_token` (JWT whose claims match the role idp_mapping) before running.

request.description: model_policies cannot be set on create; bind registries first, then update the role.
model_policies may only reference registries already attached to the role.
Validates IDP-issued JWTs; claims are matched against role idp_mapping rules. Role-based consumers only.
Role-based consumers authorize through roles and authenticate via IDP (or oauth2_client). They cannot hold registries, lb_config or fallback.

header.description: IDP-issued JWT whose claims satisfy the role idp_mapping.
Gateway discovery in header mode; in subdomain mode use Host {slug}.<GATEWAY_BASE_DOMAIN> instead.
